mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial output indicate expanding productive capacity and strong economic momentum, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO level with 60-day forward returns is non-positive over any 2-year rolling window during the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below a key threshold signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which leads to positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE crosses below 8.5%, subsequent 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances or mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in both housing starts and retail sales indicate strong household demand and economic vitality, which broadly supports corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of both rising HOUST and RSAFS does not yield a positive average 20-day return significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound, further monetary easing is constrained, and the continued low rate signals persistent economic weakness rather than accommodation, leading to negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the mean 20-day forward return is not significantly less than zero (t-test p>0.10) or positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the consumer price index signals rising inflation expectations that may prompt anticipatory monetary tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations and leads to short-term negative returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.5% month-over-month, subsequent 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases or mean return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in nonfarm payroll employment reflect improving labor market conditions that boost aggregate demand and corporate revenues, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS level with 20-day forward returns is non-positive over any 2-year rolling window during the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the effective zero lower bound and nonfarm payroll employment is rising, the combination of ultra-loose monetary policy and strengthening labor demand creates a Goldilocks environment that is strongly supportive for equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold (FEDFUNDS <=0.25% and PAYEMS rises month-over-month), 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle recovery phases, simultaneous month-over-month growth in industrial production and retail sales signals broad-based economic acceleration that strongly lifts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: During NBER-dated early-cycle expansions, the joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS does not produce a positive average 60-day return significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.10).
